content:
✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: I accept this contribution because it's a simple problem involving the use of conditionals, string manipulation for extracting hours and minutes (and converting them to cycles), and output handling when combining two different strings. Despite having several phases, this programming problem can be solved quickly and in just a few lines.

✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: Clear and fun

✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: Très fun

Seems like a good idea to me, testing input reading, conditions and perhaps my sleep schedule.
However, I would change a few things:

Goal:
* I think that strings "Lack of sleep", "Perfect sleep", "Too much sleep", "Perfect timing", "Decent timing" and "Bad timing" should be marked as constants.
* I would change "6h to 9h" to "between 6 and 9 hours" for consistency with using "hours" in the other two cases.

Input:
* Consider adding "for example", "e. g." or something similar to your examples of the input format.

Goal
* Please follow up the two points suggested by @AdSm.

Input
* Please remove all examples from the Input section, as an example is already shown in the Example section.

* Please format "HHhMM" as "[[HH]]{{h}}[[MM]]".

Output
* Please change "Line1" to "Line 1".

* Please format "," as a {{constant}}.

Constraints
* Please add lower and upper bounds for the sleep duration, e.g.

00 ≤ [[HH]] ≤ 23
00 ≤ [[MM]] ≤ 59

Cases
* Lack & Decent (Test 5): Please modify [[MM]] to be between 01 and 09. (If necessary, modify [[HH]] as well so that the expected output remains unchanged.)

* Lack & Bad (Test 6): Please change [[MM]] to a value not used by other cases.

* Too much & Bad (Test 9): Please change [[MM]] to a value not used by other cases.

Thanks.
Thanks for the feedback. I've made the changes and updated the tests.
